creative_brief: Clean, story-driven layout with Rachel's farm photo. Emphasize the contrast between supermarket rejection and direct-to-family success.
subject: Rachel's beef was too good for supermarkets
preheader: So she started selling direct. Now Sydney families get pasture-raised beef days old, not weeks.
body:
Last month, Rachel Ward watched the supermarket buyer taste her grass-fed beef and say "this is exceptional" — then offer her 40% less than it cost to raise.

That's when she decided to cut out the middlemen entirely.

Now her pasture-raised cattle from the NSW coast go straight to Sydney families via FarmThru's Brookvale hub. No 2,400km journeys. No 14-21 days in cold storage. Just grass-fed beef that's days old, not weeks old.

The difference? You can taste it in the first bite. Rich, clean flavour that only comes from cattle raised on regenerative pastures — no hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing.

This is what supermarkets can't offer: beef with a story, a farmer you can name, and quality that speaks for itself.

Rachel's beef arrives at our Brookvale hub Monday-Friday. Order online, collect fresh. It's that simple.

Want to try what the supermarket buyer called "exceptional"? Join the waitlist for early access when we launch — just a $5 refundable deposit holds your spot.

We can't promise this will change how you think about beef. But Rachel's story suggests it might.
